channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: Xue-Jie-San alleviates Crohn's disease through suppressing endothelial cell pyroptosis via the TLR4/NF-κB/NLRP3 pathway.
item.description: Journal of ethnopharmacology - Chinese herbs with the effects of promoting blood circulation and removing stasis play a vital role in CD treatment. However, research on the mechanisms by which they work is rare. Xue-Jie-San (XJS...

item.title: Corrigendum to "SDH, a novel diarylheptane compound, is a potential treatment for inflammatory bowel disease by restoring epithelial barrier function" [Biomed. Pharmacother. 151 (2022) 113080].
Goblet of fire: how Chlamydia ignites region-specific colitis by hijacking goblet cells.
item.description: The Journal of clinical investigation - Crohn's disease can occur anywhere along the small and/or large intestines, but most commonly occurs in the terminal ileum or ascending colon. Factors governing this region-specific inflammation ar...

item.title: Ginsenoside compound K up-regulates β-subunits of long-chain L-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA (HADHB) to promote fatty acid oxidation recovery and improve the balance of macrophage polarization in mice with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: British journal of pharmacology - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is characterized by mucosal inflammation and a range of clinical symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhoea and bloody stools. Evidence indicates that UC is associated wi...

item.title: Microplastics released from dental materials induce oral inflammatory bone resorption and apoptosis via mitochondrial dysfunction.
item.description: Environment international - Microplastics (MPs) are emerging pollutants that are associated with many diseases including atherosclerosis, inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and Alzheimer's. The oral cavity is the primary point...

item.title: Irgm1 Restrains CD8+ T Cell Cytokine Production and Apoptosis via Cell-Extrinsic Regulation of Type I Interferon Signaling.
item.description: European journal of immunology - Immunity-related GTPases (IRGs) are a family of proteins that maintain cellular homeostasis by promoting autophagy and mitophagy. Mutations in human IRGM and genetic deletion of mouse Irgm1 have be...

item.title: Electroacupuncture at ST36 protects against dextran sulfate sodium-induced ulcerative colitis in mice by enhancing sympathetic nerve innervation in colonic tissue.
item.description: MedScience - Ulcerative colitis (UC), characterized by diffuse colonic inflammation and enteric nervous system (ENS) alterations, remains a therapeutic challenge. While the role of the parasympathetic nervous s...
